assertions.expectation: Explains that regional clusters have multiple control plane replicas and the upgrade is rolling
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that regional clusters use multiple control plane replicas across zones and upgrades are performed in a rolling fashion with replicas upgraded one at a time

assertions.expectation: States that regional control plane upgrades have near-zero API server downtime
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Zero API server downtime!' and explains that the load balancer continues routing to healthy replicas during the rolling upgrade

assertions.expectation: Contrasts with zonal clusters where there IS a brief API server outage during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention zonal clusters or contrast them with regional clusters regarding downtime during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for control plane upgrade (--master flag)
assertions.reason: Response provides the correct gcloud command with --master flag: 'gcloud container clusters upgrade YOUR_CLUSTER_NAME --region=us-central1 --master --cluster-version=1.30.X-gke.XXXX'

assertions.expectation: Mentions expected duration for control plane upgrade (roughly 10-30 minutes)
assertions.reason: Response states 'The upgrade typically takes 10-30 minutes' in the additional safety recommendations section

assertions.expectation: Includes a command to verify the control plane version after upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl version --short' command in the 'After control plane upgrade' section to verify the control plane version

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking kube-system pods health after upgrade
assertions.reason: While the response mentions checking pods with 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep -v Running', it doesn't specifically mention kube-system pods or checking their health after the upgrade

assertions.expectation: Advises checking deprecated API usage before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get apiservices --sort-by=.metadata.name' command in the 'Before the upgrade' section to check for deprecated APIs
